title: What feeling is asking to be heard tonight?
subtitle: A journal set for naming what the day has left in the body before sleep.
excerpt: A journal prompt set for Day 6 of the SLEEP program. Five prompts for naming what the day left in the body — not to solve it, but to give it enough acknowledgement that it can rest.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Developed as a companion practice to Day 6 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use in the hour before bed when you are aware of a feeling that hasn't been fully processed — something left over from the day that you don't want to carry into sleep.

content.text: A journal set for Day 6 of the SLEEP program: naming what the day has left behind. Not a problem-solving exercise — a noticing one. The feeling doesn't need to be resolved before sleep; it needs to be acknowledged.
New to journal prompts as a practice? Read
What feeling is present in my body right now?
Name it plainly. Not the story, not the explanation — just the word. Tired. Irritated. Sad. Buzzing. Write what is actually there.
Where in my body do I feel it?
Chest, throat, jaw, shoulders, stomach. Be specific.
What happened today that this feeling is connected to?
One moment. Not a full account of the day — the thing that is still there.
Does this feeling need anything from me tonight?
Not what it wants you to do tomorrow — what it needs now, before sleep. Action, acknowledgement, or nothing at all.
What would it feel like to set this down until morning?
Write one sentence about what it would be like to put it down rather than carry it in. Not to dismiss it — to let it rest.
Original to homemade.education. Developed as a companion practice to Day 6 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
